LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS TEVA SALES AND MARKETING INC AND EYAD MATAR'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS TEVA SALES AND MARKETING, INC. AND EYAD MATAR'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF K. NIELSEN'S COMPLAINT IS DENIED. UNDER LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT (1996) 12 CAL.4TH 631, A PLAINTIFF CAN SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES IF AN EMPLOYER FRAUDULENTLY INDUCES HER TO ENTER INTO AN EMPLOYMENT CONTRACT BECAUSE BY "PURSUING A VALID FRAUD ACTION, A PLAINTIFF ADVANCES THE PUBLIC INTEREST IN PUNISHING INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATIONS AND IN DETERRING SUCH MISREPRESENTATIONS IN THE FUTURE." (ID. AT 639.) UNLIKE BREWER V. PREMIER GOLF PROPERTIES, LP (2008) 168 CAL.APP.4TH 1243, 1250, THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT SEEK PUNITIVE DAMAGES BASED SOLELY ON A LABOR CODE VIOLATION; RATHER, THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARE SOUGHT FOR THE ALLEGED FRAUDULENT INDUCEMENT TO ACCEPT DEFENDANTS' OFFER OF EMPLOYMENT BY THE USE OF FALSE PROMISES. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF IS ALLOWED TO PLEAD ALTERNATIVE THEORIES OF RECOVERY. (SEE CORTEZ V. PUROLATOR AIR FILTRATION PRODUCTS CO. (2000) 23 CAL.4TH 163, 174 ["UNPAID WAGES MIGHT BE RECOVERED AS DAMAGES IN A CIVIL SUIT FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT OR ONE PREMISED ON FRAUD OR MISREPRESENTATION THEORIES. IT DOES NOT FOLLOW, HOWEVER, THAT WHEN THE FAILURE TO PAY WAGES VIOLATES THE LABOR CODE AND THEREFORE CONSTITUTES AN UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICE THE WAGES OWED MAY NOT BE RECOVERED AS RESTITUTION IN A UCL ACTION."].) (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel L. Feder, esq. (415-391-9476) for Plaintiff via court call.
M. Leah Cameron and Candace Des Baillets, esq. (415-981-3233) of Carothers Disante & Freudenberger LLP for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs TEVA SALES AND MARKETING INC and EYAD MATAR's DEMURRER TO plaintiff's COMPLAINT.

Defendants Teva Sales and Marketing, Inc. and Eyad Matar's demurrer to the first through fifth causes of action of plaintiff K. Nielsen's complaint is sustained with 20 days' leave to amend. (see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendant Teva Sales And Marketing Inc, Eyad Matar Demurrer To Complaint (Tentative Ruling 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Daniel L. Feder, esq. (415-391-9476) for Plaintiff via court call.
M. Leah Cameron and Candace Des Baillets, esq. (415-981-3233) of Carothers Disante & Freudenberger LLP for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs TEVA SALES AND MARKETING INC and EYAD MATAR's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants Teva Sales and Marketing, Inc. and Eyad Matar's motion to strike portions of plaintiff K. Nielsen's complaint is denied. Under Lazar v. Superior Court (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, a plaintiff can seek punitive damages if an employer fraudulently induces her to enter into an employment contract because by "pursuing a valid fraud action, a plaintiff advances the public interest in punishing intentional misrepresentations and in deterring such misrepresentations in the future." (Id. at 639.) Unlike Brewer v. Premier Golf Properties, LP (2008) 168 Cal.App.4th 1243, 1250, the complaint does not seek punitive damages based solely on a Labor Code violation; rather, the punitive damages are sought for the alleged fraudulent inducement to accept defendants' offer of employment by the use of false promises. Further, plaintiff is allowed to plead alternative theories of recovery. (See Cortez v. Purolator Air Filtration Products Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 163, 174 ["unpaid wages might be recovered as damages in a civil suit for breach of contract or one premised on fraud or misrepresentation theories. It does not follow, however, that when the failure to pay wages violates the Labor Code and therefore constitutes an unfair business practice the wages owed may not be recovered as restitution in a UCL action."].) (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
